title: Recording provider for the dumb agent
prompt: Which token source should the deliberately-dumb recording agent use when capturing real fixture runs (phase 3)?
context: The provider stays pluggable either way, and the scripted (deterministic, no-LLM) provider is always phase 1's spine + CI's fast path. This choice is only about the *real-model* recorder that captures the committed fixtures the website replays. Recording is an occasional dev-machine act (fixture regeneration), not per-CI-run, so cost scales with re-records, not commits. June's decision said "a real local-or-cheap-API dumb model" without pinning which.

options.label: Cheap API model — claude-haiku (Recommended)
options.description: Real tool-calling reliability with a genuinely weak-enough model; zero local setup; pennies per recorded run since recording is occasional. Highest-fidelity signal per unit of harness work.

options.label: Local small model (no API dependency)
options.description: Free and offline, but flakier tool-calling means more harness babysitting, and the harness work to stabilize it lands before the protocol signal does.

options.label: Defer the real model — scripted provider only for now
options.description: Ship spine + player on scripted fixtures alone; pick the real-model provider only when the replay surface has proven out. Lowest scope now, but protocol fidelity stays untested against a real model.
